rationale: Standard choice for a low-latency session cache; sub-millisecond reads

decision: Move the session cache from Redis to a Postgres UNLOGGED table
rationale: UNLOGGED benchmarked within 15% of Redis for our read pattern, and drops a second datastore to provision, monitor, back up and secure

rejected.reason: A second datastore's ops and on-call burden for a 15% read win we don't need

rationale: Industry-standard orchestration; room to grow into multi-service

decision: Run all backend services on ECS Fargate
rationale: Two engineers cannot carry cluster upgrades, CNI debugging and node pools; Fargate covers every workload we actually run

rejected.reason: Operational surface far beyond what this team can staff

decision: Expose the public API as GraphQL
rationale: Clients pick their own fields; one endpoint for every screen

rejected.reason: Mobile screens overfetch whole resources to read two fields

decision: Migrate the public API from GraphQL back to REST with sparse fieldsets
rationale: Resolver N+1s and cache invalidation cost more than the flexibility bought us; ?fields= covers the overfetching complaint at a fraction of the complexity

rejected.reason: Resolver N+1s, no HTTP caching, and a schema only two people could safely change

rationale: Sub-second cold starts and a simpler config than the alternatives

rejected.reason: Smaller plugin ecosystem for our needs

decision: Bundle the dashboard with Webpack, using Module Federation for embedded entry points
rationale: The partner-facing shell requires Module Federation at runtime; the Vite federation plugin was not production-ready when we needed it

rejected.reason: Experimental; broke shared-dependency versioning in the spike

decision: Structured logging with pino, one JSON line per request
rationale: Fast, structured, and the ops dashboards parse JSON lines natively

decision: All API errors use the problem+json envelope
rationale: RFC 7807 gives clients one error shape; internal codes map to documented types

decision: Zod schemas validate every API input at the boundary
rationale: One schema per endpoint doubles as the OpenAPI source of truth

decision: OpenTelemetry traces exported to the collector, sampled at 10%
rationale: Vendor-neutral instrumentation; sampling keeps ingest costs flat
Strict node_modules layout catches phantom dependencies

decision: User file uploads go to S3-compatible object storage, never the database
rationale: Keeps the database small and backups fast; presigned URLs offload transfer
